title: Fed Meeting Underscores Tough Task Ahead for Warsh
summary: Jerome H. Powell on Wednesday announced he would stay on as a governor at the central bank as internal divisions sharpen about the policy path forward.

title: Powell Says He Will Stay On as Fed Governor After Term as Chair Ends
summary: Jerome H. Powell cited lingering legal threats against him and the Federal Reserve in explaining his decision to remain at the central bank.

title: Supreme Court Limits Reach of Voting Rights Act
summary: Also, the Pentagon estimates the Iran war cost at $25 billion. Here’s the latest at the end of Wednesday.

title: Supreme Court Strikes Down Louisiana Map, Another Blow to Voting Rights Act
summary: The court struck down the voting map as an unconstitutional racial gerrymander in a move that could make it harder for lawmakers to create majority-minority voting districts.

title: What the Supreme Court’s Voting Rights Ruling Could Mean for the Midterms
summary: Democrats stand to lose at least one blue-leaning district in Louisiana, but the timing was unclear. Florida has approved a redder map, and Republicans in several other states are weighing new districts.

title: Civil rights leaders see the voting rights ruling as a ‘betrayal.’
summary: Leaders and activists said a Supreme Court ruling had the potential to unravel decades of work toward giving Black Americans fair representation.

title: What the Royal State Dinner Guest List Says About Trump’s America
summary: There were at least 10 American billionaires, six Fox News hosts, assorted presidential pals, no Democratic politicians and not so many British.

title: Mamdani’s Advice to Royals: Give That Priceless Diamond Back to India
summary: Mayor Zohran Mamdani and King Charles did not meet privately. But if they had, the mayor said, he probably would have raised the issue of the Koh-i-Noor Diamond.

title: Queen Camilla Unites Winnie-the-Pooh With a Long-Lost Friend
summary: On Wednesday, the Queen of England presented the New York Public Library with a bespoke replica of Roo, the smallest companion of the Bear of Very Little Brain.

title: Pentagon Puts Iran War Cost at $25 Billion as Hegseth Berates Skeptics
summary: During his first public appearance on Capitol Hill since the war began, the defense secretary lashed out at lawmakers in both parties who have questioned the conflict.

title: Hegseth Cites Falsehood to Defend His Firing of Senior Officers
summary: The defense secretary said at a House hearing that President Barack Obama had fired 197 generals, a figure that the Pentagon previously acknowledged was false.

title: Trump Threatens to Pull Troops From Germany as He Lashes Out at Merz
summary: The comments came after the German chancellor said that Iran has “humiliated” the United States.

title: Lucinda Williams Explains What It Takes to Write a Great Song
summary: The singer-songwriter talks about being self-taught, and reaching down into the deepest, darkest parts of herself to pull out a song.

title: How Taylor Swift Writes a Song, In Her Own Words
summary: The artist shares stories behind some of her biggest hits, her love of a “rant bridge” and how life in the public eye informs the stories she tells in her songs.

title: Surveillance Law Is in Limbo After House Approval
summary: Republicans put down a right-wing revolt to push a three-year renewal through the House, but the Senate appeared likely to opt for a 45-day punt ahead of a Friday expiration.

title: White House Correspondents’ Dinner Shooter Planned Attack Weeks Before Gala, Prosecutors Say
summary: A new prosecution memo details the preparations made in the days and weeks before the assault on the White House correspondents’ dinner.

title: They Left for the School Bus. ICE Picked Them Up Instead.
summary: A school transfer disrupted two brothers’ visas, their lawyer said, leaving them vulnerable to arrest and unsettling their Mississippi school community.

title: G.O.P. Congress Struggles to Do the Basics Amid Party Infighting
summary: House Republicans toiled to pass major measures as lawmakers scrambled to fund homeland security, extend spy powers and deliver a farm bill.

title: Why Can’t Parkland Be Used as a Park? Ask the Judges Who Park There.
summary: A decades-old fight over a parking lot reserved for judges in Brooklyn has picked up steam with a new generation of combatants.

title: Trump and Putin Call for a Brief Cease-Fire in Ukraine
summary: Previous truces have broken down amid competing accusations of violations, and it is not clear that Ukraine will agree to Moscow’s terms.

title: ‘Excruciating and Agonizing’: A New Reality for Jewish Democrats
summary: With Israel increasingly unpopular and antisemitism on the rise, Jewish politicians find themselves more and more under attack.

title: The Murky Ethics of Swimming With Killer Whales
summary: Only two places in the world allow tourists to enter the water with the ocean’s apex predator. But the safety of both species is a growing concern.

title: Musk Says He ‘Was a Fool’ to Provide OpenAI’s Early Funding
summary: In the second day of a trial pitting Mr. Musk against OpenAI, he said the company’s chief executive, Sam Altman, had misled him. But OpenAI’s lawyer said evidence showed the opposite.

title: A.I. Spending Sets a Record, With No End in Sight
summary: Google, Amazon, Microsoft and Meta reported more than $130 billion in quarterly capital expenditures on Wednesday as they build A.I. data centers. There’s more to come.

title: Meta Deal Reversal Deepens Split Between China and Silicon Valley
summary: Beijing’s insistence that Meta unwind its deal with a Chinese A.I. start-up escalates the geopolitical fight over advanced tech.

title: The Justices Acted as Partisans in the Voting Rights Ruling
summary: In the name of disentangling race from politics, the court has given white voters more power at the expense of racial minorities.

title: Two Cocky Authoritarians Blocking Hormuz: What Could Go Wrong?
summary: In the Iran war, we have two overconfident administrations facing off, each believing that time is on its side.

title: U.S. Indictment Accuses Mexican Governor of Conspiring With Sinaloa Cartel
summary: Prosecutors accused Rubén Rocha Moya, the governor of Sinaloa state, and other Mexican officials of a yearslong conspiracy to protect the powerful cartel.

title: Brown University Gunman Planned Attack for Years, F.B.I. Says
summary: Investigators said the man’s transient lifestyle and social isolation made his intentions hard to track before shootings that killed two Brown students and an M.I.T. professor.

title: Mississippi Middle School Students Avert Bus Crash After Driver Loses Control
summary: Footage of the incident shared this week by a school district in Mississippi shows a group of students working together to avert disaster on a highway.

title: Supreme Court Grapples With Trump’s Plan to Revoke Deportation Protections
summary: The case deals with Temporary Protected Status for hundreds of thousands of Haitians and Syrians but could have implications for more than a million from troubled nations.
